name: Author workflow
content:
You are authoring an S.E.E. workflow definition (JSON) for the engine.

Goals:
- Turn the operator request into a valid workflow bundle: outer envelope (`id` like `system-<kebab>`, `name`, `description`, `version`, `content`) plus inner `content` per `core/schema/workflow.schema.json`.
- Prefer saving as a new file under `.s_e_e/workflows/definitions/<slug>.json` in the hub (on-disk filename does not identify the workflow; JSON `id` and `content.id` do).

Rules (must):
- Validate against `core/schema/workflow.schema.json` before declaring done.
- Declare every `\{{runtime.<key>}}` under `content.runtime_inputs`; keys must not contain dots.
- Every `\{{userinput.<task_id>.value}}` must reference a `user_input` task with that exact `id` that runs earlier in the graph.
- Set `content.spoke_root` and/or per-task `spoke` on every `command`, `mise_gate`, and `git_action` task as required.
- Prefer `\{{prompt.<namespace>-<id>}}` for long reusable instructions; keep `command.input.prompt` as glue plus placeholders.
- Never put secrets, tokens, or API keys in workflow JSON.
- Exactly one handler key per task `function` object (`oneOf`).

References:
- `.s_e_e/knowledge/docs/overview/workflow-engine/doc-18 - 18-Workflow-Authoring-Guide.md`
- `.s_e_e/knowledge/docs/overview/workflow-engine/doc-13 - 13-Prompts-and-Workflow-Template-Expansion.md`

Deliverable: concrete workflow JSON (or precise edits) implementing the operator request; list files touched.
